title: Three Dimensional Geometry
quote: The moving power of mathematical invention is not reasoning but imagination. -- A. DEMORGAN

sections.title: Introduction
concepts: Use of vector algebra in three dimensional geometry makes the study simple and elegant
Direction cosines and direction ratios of a line joining two points
Equations of lines and planes in space under different conditions
Angle between two lines, two planes, a line and a plane
Shortest distance between two skew lines
Distance of a point from a plane
Results obtained in vector form and translated to Cartesian form
key_points: In Class XI, Analytical Geometry in two dimensions and introduction to three dimensional geometry used Cartesian methods only
This chapter uses vector algebra for 3D geometry

sections.title: Direction Cosines and Direction Ratios of a Line
concepts: Direction angles: angles alpha, beta, gamma that a directed line L makes with x, y, z axes
Direction cosines: cos(alpha), cos(beta), cos(gamma) denoted by l, m, n
Reversing direction of L replaces angles by their supplements, reversing signs of direction cosines
A line in space has two sets of direction cosines (opposite directions)
For a unique set, the line must be taken as a directed line
Direction ratios: any three numbers proportional to direction cosines

definitions.definition: If a directed line L passes through the origin making angles alpha, beta, gamma with x, y, z axes respectively, then cos(alpha), cos(beta), cos(gamma) are called direction cosines, denoted by l, m, n

Any three numbers a, b, c which are proportional to the direction cosines l, m, n of a line. If l, m, n are direction cosines, then a = lambda*l, b = lambda*m, c = lambda*n for any nonzero lambda in R

formulas.name: Direction cosines identity
formulas.description: Sum of squares of direction cosines equals 1

formulas.name: Relation between direction ratios and direction cosines
formulas.formula: l/a = m/b = n/c = k, where k = +/- 1/sqrt(a^2 + b^2 + c^2)
formulas.description: Connecting direction ratios (a,b,c) to direction cosines (l,m,n)

formulas.name: Direction cosines from direction ratios
formulas.description: Direction cosines computed from direction ratios

formulas.name: Direction cosines of line joining two points
formulas.description: Direction cosines of line segment joining P(x1,y1,z1) and Q(x2,y2,z2)

formulas.name: Direction ratios of line joining two points
formulas.description: Direction ratios of line segment from P(x1,y1,z1) to Q(x2,y2,z2)

key_points: For any line, if a, b, c are direction ratios, then ka, kb, kc (k != 0) are also direction ratios
Any two sets of direction ratios of a line are proportional
There are infinitely many sets of direction ratios for any line
Two parallel lines have the same set of direction cosines
Direction cosines of x-axis: 1, 0, 0; y-axis: 0, 1, 0; z-axis: 0, 0, 1

subsections.title: Direction cosines of a line passing through two points
subsections.description: Since one and only one line passes through two given points, we can determine the direction cosines of a line passing through points P(x1,y1,z1) and Q(x2,y2,z2)

examples.statement: If a line makes angle 90 deg, 60 deg and 30 deg with the positive direction of x, y and z-axis respectively, find its direction cosines.
examples.answer: l = cos 90 = 0, m = cos 60 = 1/2, n = cos 30 = sqrt(3)/2

examples.statement: If a line has direction ratios 2, -1, -2, determine its direction cosines.
Find the direction cosines of the line passing through the two points (-2, 4, -5) and (1, 2, 3).
Find the direction cosines of x, y and z-axis.
Show that the points A(2, 3, -4), B(1, -2, 3) and C(3, 8, -11) are collinear.
examples.answer: Direction ratios of AB are -1, -5, 7 and of BC are 2, 10, -14 which are proportional, hence collinear.

sections.title: Equation of a Line in Space
concepts: A line is uniquely determined if (i) it passes through a given point and has given direction, or (ii) it passes through two given points
Vector equation of a line through a point parallel to a given vector
Cartesian equation of a line (symmetric form)
Parametric equations of a line
Equation of line passing through two given points

formulas.name: Vector equation of a line (point + direction)
formulas.description: Line through point with position vector a, parallel to vector b. lambda is a real parameter.

formulas.name: Cartesian equation of a line (point + direction ratios)
formulas.description: Line through (x1,y1,z1) with direction ratios a, b, c

formulas.name: Cartesian equation using direction cosines
formulas.description: Line through (x1,y1,z1) with direction cosines l, m, n

formulas.name: Parametric equations of a line
formulas.formula: x = x1 + lambda*a, y = y1 + lambda*b, z = z1 + lambda*c
formulas.description: Parametric form of line through (x1,y1,z1) with direction ratios a, b, c

formulas.name: Vector equation of a line through two points
formulas.description: Line through two points with position vectors a and b

key_points: If b = a*i_hat + b*j_hat + c*k_hat, then a, b, c are direction ratios of the line and conversely
b should not be confused with |b|
Derivation of Cartesian form from vector form: eliminate lambda from parametric equations

subsections.title: Equation of a line through a given point and parallel to a given vector b
subsections.description: Let a be the position vector of point A. Line through A parallel to b has vector equation r = a + lambda*b

subsections.title: Equation of a line passing through two given points
subsections.description: Line through two points with position vectors a and b has equation r = a + lambda*(b - a)

examples.statement: Find the vector and the Cartesian equations of the line through the point (5, 2, -4) and which is parallel to the vector 3i_hat + 2j_hat - 8k_hat.

sections.title: Angle between Two Lines
concepts: Angle between two lines using direction ratios
Angle between two lines using direction cosines
Angle between lines in vector form
Conditions for perpendicularity and parallelism of two lines

formulas.name: Angle between two lines (direction ratios)
formulas.description: Angle between lines with direction ratios (a1,b1,c1) and (a2,b2,c2)

formulas.name: Angle between two lines (direction cosines)
formulas.description: Angle between lines with direction cosines (l1,m1,n1) and (l2,m2,n2), since l^2+m^2+n^2=1

formulas.name: sin(theta) using direction ratios
formulas.description: Sine of angle between two lines

formulas.name: sin(theta) using direction cosines
formulas.description: Sine of angle between two lines using direction cosines

formulas.name: Angle between lines in vector form
formulas.description: For lines r = a1 + lambda*b1 and r = a2 + mu*b2

formulas.name: Condition for perpendicular lines (direction ratios)
formulas.description: Two lines are perpendicular when theta = 90 deg

formulas.name: Condition for parallel lines (direction ratios)
formulas.description: Two lines are parallel when theta = 0

key_points: If lines do not pass through origin, take parallel lines through origin to find angle
The angle theta is always taken as the acute angle between the lines

examples.statement: Find the angle between the pair of lines r = 3i_hat + 2j_hat - 4k_hat + lambda(i_hat + 2j_hat + 2k_hat) and r = 5i_hat - 2j_hat + mu(3i_hat + 2j_hat + 6k_hat).
Find the angle between the pair of lines (x+3)/3 = (y-1)/5 = (z+3)/4 and (x+1)/1 = (y-4)/1 = (z-5)/2.

sections.title: Shortest Distance between Two Lines
concepts: Shortest distance between intersecting lines is zero
Shortest distance between parallel lines is the perpendicular distance
Skew lines: lines that are neither parallel nor intersecting (non-coplanar)
The shortest distance between skew lines is perpendicular to both lines

definitions.definition: Lines in space which are neither intersecting nor parallel. Such pair of lines are non coplanar.
The join of a point in one line with one point on the other line so that the length of the segment so obtained is the smallest.

formulas.name: Shortest distance between skew lines (vector form)
formulas.description: For lines r = a1 + lambda*b1 and r = a2 + mu*b2

formulas.name: Shortest distance between skew lines (Cartesian form)
formulas.description: For lines (x-x1)/a1 = (y-y1)/b1 = (z-z1)/c1 and (x-x2)/a2 = (y-y2)/b2 = (z-z2)/c2

formulas.name: Distance between parallel lines
formulas.description: For parallel lines r = a1 + lambda*b and r = a2 + mu*b

key_points: For skew lines, the line of shortest distance is perpendicular to both lines
The unit vector along the shortest distance is n_hat = (b1 x b2)/|b1 x b2|
If two lines intersect, the shortest distance is zero
If two lines are parallel, they are coplanar

subsections.title: Distance between two skew lines
subsections.description: Derivation of shortest distance formula using cross product and projection

subsections.title: Distance between parallel lines
subsections.description: For parallel lines, the distance equals |b x (a2-a1)|/|b|

examples.statement: Find the shortest distance between the lines l1 and l2 whose vector equations are r = i_hat + j_hat + lambda(2i_hat - j_hat + k_hat) and r = 2i_hat + j_hat - k_hat + mu(3i_hat - 5j_hat + 2k_hat).
examples.answer: a2 - a1 = i_hat - k_hat, b1 x b2 = 3i_hat - j_hat - 7k_hat, |b1 x b2| = sqrt(59), d = |3-0+7|/sqrt(59) = 10/sqrt(59)

examples.statement: Find the distance between the lines l1 and l2 given by r = i_hat + 2j_hat - 4k_hat + lambda(2i_hat + 3j_hat + 6k_hat) and r = 3i_hat + 3j_hat - 5k_hat + mu(2i_hat + 3j_hat + 6k_hat).
examples.answer: Lines are parallel. a2 - a1 = 2i_hat + j_hat - k_hat, b = 2i_hat + 3j_hat + 6k_hat. d = |-9i_hat + 14j_hat - 4k_hat|/sqrt(49) = sqrt(293)/7

summary: Direction cosines of a line are the cosines of the angles made by the line with the positive directions of the coordinate axes.
If l, m, n are the direction cosines of a line, then l^2 + m^2 + n^2 = 1.
Direction cosines of a line joining two points P(x1,y1,z1) and Q(x2,y2,z2) are (x2-x1)/PQ, (y2-y1)/PQ, (z2-z1)/PQ where PQ = sqrt((x2-x1)^2 + (y2-y1)^2 + (z2-z1)^2).
Direction ratios of a line are the numbers which are proportional to the direction cosines of a line.
If l, m, n are the direction cosines and a, b, c are the direction ratios of a line then l = a/sqrt(a^2+b^2+c^2), m = b/sqrt(a^2+b^2+c^2), n = c/sqrt(a^2+b^2+c^2).
Skew lines are lines in space which are neither parallel nor intersecting. They lie in different planes.
Angle between skew lines is the angle between two intersecting lines drawn from any point (preferably through the origin) parallel to each of the skew lines.
If l1, m1, n1 and l2, m2, n2 are the direction cosines of two lines and theta is the acute angle between them, then cos(theta) = |l1*l2 + m1*m2 + n1*n2|.

If a1, b1, c1 and a2, b2, c2 are the direction ratios of two lines and theta is the acute angle between them, then cos(theta) = |a1*a2 + b1*b2 + c1*c2| / (sqrt(a1^2+b1^2+c1^2) * sqrt(a2^2+b2^2+c2^2)).
Vector equation of a line through a point with position vector a and parallel to vector b is r = a + lambda*b.
Equation of a line through point (x1,y1,z1) having direction cosines l, m, n is (x-x1)/l = (y-y1)/m = (z-z1)/n.
The vector equation of a line passing through two points with position vectors a and b is r = a + lambda*(b - a).
If theta is the acute angle between r = a1 + lambda*b1 and r = a2 + mu*b2, then cos(theta) = |b1 . b2| / (|b1| * |b2|).
If (x-x1)/l1 = (y-y1)/m1 = (z-z1)/n1 and (x-x2)/l2 = (y-y2)/m2 = (z-z2)/n2 are the equations of two lines, then the acute angle between them is given by cos(theta) = |l1*l2 + m1*m2 + n1*n2|.
Shortest distance between two skew lines is the line segment perpendicular to both the lines.
Shortest distance between r = a1 + lambda*b1 and r = a2 + mu*b2 is |(b1 x b2) . (a2 - a1)| / |b1 x b2|.

Shortest distance between lines (x-x1)/a1 = (y-y1)/b1 = (z-z1)/c1 and (x-x2)/a2 = (y-y2)/b2 = (z-z2)/c2 is |det[[x2-x1,y2-y1,z2-z1],[a1,b1,c1],[a2,b2,c2]]| / sqrt((b1*c2-b2*c1)^2+(c1*a2-c2*a1)^2+(a1*b2-a2*b1)^2).
Distance between parallel lines r = a1 + lambda*b and r = a2 + mu*b is |b x (a2 - a1)| / |b|.

questions.question: If a line makes angles 90 deg, 135 deg, 45 deg with the x, y and z-axes respectively, find its direction cosines.
Find the direction cosines of a line which makes equal angles with the coordinate axes.
If a line has the direction ratios -18, 12, -4, then what are its direction cosines?
Show that the points (2, 3, 4), (-1, -2, 1), (5, 8, 7) are collinear.
Find the direction cosines of the sides of the triangle whose vertices are (3, 5, -4), (-1, 1, 2) and (-5, -5, -2).
Show that the three lines with direction cosines 12/13, -3/13, -4/13; 4/13, 12/13, 3/13; 3/13, -4/13, 12/13 are mutually perpendicular.
Show that the line through the points (1, -1, 2), (3, 4, -2) is perpendicular to the line through the points (0, 3, 2) and (3, 5, 6).
Show that the line through the points (4, 7, 8), (2, 3, 4) is parallel to the line through the points (-1, -2, 1), (1, 2, 5).
Find the equation of the line which passes through the point (1, 2, 3) and is parallel to the vector 3i_hat + 2j_hat - 2k_hat.
Find the equation of the line in vector and in cartesian form that passes through the point with position vector 2i_hat - j_hat + 4k_hat and is in the direction i_hat + 2j_hat - k_hat.
Find the cartesian equation of the line which passes through the point (-2, 4, -5) and parallel to the line given by (x+3)/3 = (y-4)/5 = (z+8)/6.
The cartesian equation of a line is (x-5)/3 = (y+4)/7 = (z-6)/2. Write its vector form.
Find the angle between the following pairs of lines:

parts.question: r = 2i_hat - 5j_hat + k_hat + lambda(3i_hat + 2j_hat + 6k_hat) and r = 7i_hat - 6k_hat + mu(i_hat + 2j_hat + 2k_hat)
r = 3i_hat + j_hat - 2k_hat + lambda(i_hat - j_hat - 2k_hat) and r = 2i_hat - j_hat - 56k_hat + mu(3i_hat - 5j_hat - 4k_hat)

questions.question: Find the angle between the following pair of lines:
Find the values of p so that the lines (1-x)/3 = (7y-14)/(2p) = (z-3)/2 and (7-7x)/(3p) = (y-5)/1 = (6-z)/5 are at right angles.
Show that the lines (x-5)/7 = (y+2)/(-5) = z/1 and x/1 = y/2 = z/3 are perpendicular to each other.
Find the shortest distance between the lines r = (i_hat + 2j_hat + k_hat) + lambda(i_hat - j_hat + k_hat) and r = 2i_hat - j_hat - k_hat + mu(2i_hat + j_hat + 2k_hat).
Find the shortest distance between the lines (x+1)/7 = (y+1)/(-6) = (z+1)/1 and (x-3)/1 = (y-5)/(-2) = (z-7)/1.
Find the shortest distance between the lines whose vector equations are r = (i_hat + 2j_hat + 3k_hat) + lambda(i_hat - 3j_hat + 2k_hat) and r = 4i_hat + 5j_hat + 6k_hat + mu(2i_hat + 3j_hat + k_hat).
Find the shortest distance between the lines whose vector equations are r = (1-t)i_hat + (t-2)j_hat + (3-2t)k_hat and r = (s+1)i_hat + (2s-1)j_hat - (2s+1)k_hat.
Find the angle between the lines whose direction ratios are a, b, c and b-c, c-a, a-b.
Find the equation of a line parallel to x-axis and passing through the origin.
If the lines (x-1)/(-3) = (y-2)/(2k) = (z-3)/2 and (x-1)/(3k) = (y-1)/1 = (z-6)/(-5) are perpendicular, find the value of k.
Find the shortest distance between lines r = 6i_hat + 2j_hat + 2k_hat + lambda(i_hat - 2j_hat + 2k_hat) and r = -4i_hat - k_hat + mu(3i_hat - 2j_hat - 2k_hat).
Find the vector equation of the line passing through the point (1, 2, -4) and perpendicular to the two lines: (x-8)/3 = (y+19)/(-16) = (z-10)/7 and (x-15)/3 = (y-29)/8 = (z-5)/(-5).
